raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the help focus primarily on sentence-level polishing, or on higher-level argument and claim positioning?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The section contains contribution and interpretation language that may need reframing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The delegation plausibly includes checking whether the methodological description, results, ablation, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The section makes performance and ablation claims that should be aligned with the reported WER numbers and experimental observations.","R1.6":"A broad request to help with a paper section most directly entails improving sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and conciseness.","RX.1":"Any projected help must preserve uncertainty around untuned hyperparameters, modest gains, and unisolated causal factors.","RX.2":"The section repeatedly discusses possible improvement over prior work, so avoiding overclaiming is centrally relevant.","RX.3":"The projection must stay within responsibility assignment rather than editing, rewriting, or evaluating the artifact itself.","RX.4":"Care is needed to avoid encouraging misleading claims about benchmark superiority or causal effects.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the explicit delegation and the concrete claims present in the section."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the help focus primarily on sentence-level polishing, or on higher-level argument and claim positioning?

rationale.R1.1: The section contains contribution and interpretation language that may need reframing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation plausibly includes checking whether the methodological description, results, ablation, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes performance and ablation claims that should be aligned with the reported WER numbers and experimental observations.
rationale.R1.6: A broad request to help with a paper section most directly entails improving sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and conciseness.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected help must preserve uncertainty around untuned hyperparameters, modest gains, and unisolated causal factors.
rationale.RX.2: The section repeatedly discusses possible improvement over prior work, so avoiding overclaiming is centrally relevant.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within responsibility assignment rather than editing, rewriting, or evaluating the artifact itself.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to avoid encouraging misleading claims about benchmark superiority or causal effects.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the explicit delegation and the concrete claims present in the section.
